sections.paragraph:
fut. יַחְפֹּז [“to leap or spring up, kindred to קָפַז, قفز; comp. פָּזַז, افز, وفز, ذفز”]-

(1) to flee with haste, or fright [“to spring up suddenly in order to flee”]. (Arab. causat. حفز to thrust forward, to impel. A kindred root is פָּחַד.) 2Ki 7:15 (כתיב); Job 40:23 to be in alarm, Psa 31:23 116:11 .

(2) to make haste, 2Sa 4:4. Compare Lat. fugere, trepidum esse, used of any kind of haste, Virg. Georg. iii. 462; iv. 73; so Hebr. נִבְהַל, Syr. ܙܰܪܗܶܒ to be in alarm, to cause to make haste [but see above].

Niphal-

(1) to flee, Psa 48:6 104:7 .

(2) to make haste, 1Sa 23:26. Hence-
